description: Hide the copy button by default on code blocks that have no language specified. Language-less code blocks are almost always example output, not copyable commands. This eliminates the need to manually add nocopy to dozens of code fences across the docs.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Auto-hide copy button on language-less code blocks and remove manual nocopy from dev-setup.mdx
userStories.description: As a docs reader, I want code blocks showing example output to not show a Copy button, so that the UI is clean without docs authors needing to manually annotate every output block.
acceptanceCriteria: CodeBlock in docs/components/CodeBlock.tsx hides the copy button (header bar and mobile overlay) when data-language is absent or empty, unless data-copy is explicitly set
The showCopy logic becomes: show the copy button when there IS a language OR when data-copy is explicitly present; hide it when there is no language AND data-copy is not set
The existing data-nocopy attribute is removed from CodeBlock since it is no longer needed — the default is now reversed (no-language = no copy)

The nocopy meta attribute is removed from docs/content/docs/contributing/dev-setup.mdx line 62 (change ```text nocopy back to ```text or just ```) since language-less blocks auto-hide and text blocks can use the new default behavior
The error-handling.mdx code block at line 13 (``` with Tool dispatch error) renders without a Copy button — no changes needed to the MDX file since the auto-hide handles it
Code blocks WITH a language (```bash, ```typescript, etc.) still show the Copy button as before
If a future docs author needs a copy button on a language-less block, they can add a data-copy meta attribute to force it on
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Two files need changes:

1. docs/components/CodeBlock.tsx — Change the showCopy logic:

   Current (lines 13, 18):
     'data-nocopy': nocopy,
     ...
     const showCopy = nocopy === undefined;

   New approach:
     Remove the data-nocopy extraction. Add data-copy extraction instead.
     'data-copy': forceCopy,
     ...
     const showCopy = language !== undefined || forceCopy !== undefined;

   This means:
   - Has a language (```bash, ```typescript, etc.) → show copy button
   - No language but has data-copy → show copy button (opt-in override)
   - No language and no data-copy → hide copy button (the common case for output examples)

   Update the type annotation too:
     { 'data-language'?: string; 'data-copy'?: string }

   The rest of CodeBlock stays the same — the showCopy variable already controls both the header bar and mobile overlay rendering.

2. docs/content/docs/contributing/dev-setup.mdx — Remove the nocopy meta attribute from line 62:

   Current: ```text nocopy
   New: ```text

   Since ```text has a language ('text'), it will show the copy button by default. But this is an output example — it shouldn't have a copy button. So actually, change it to just:
   ```
   (language-less, which auto-hides copy)

   Or keep ```text to have the language label. The choice depends on whether the language label adds value for output blocks. Since ```text shows 'text' in the header bar... but wait, the header bar is hidden when showCopy is false. The language label is only visible inside the header bar. So for output blocks, ```text vs ``` are visually identical when the copy button is hidden.

   Decision: Change it back to ``` (language-less) so it auto-hides. The ```text language label served no purpose since it was only visible in the now-hidden header bar.

   Actually, re-reading CodeBlock.tsx: the language label is ONLY shown inside the header bar div (line 36). When showCopy is false, the entire header bar is hidden, so the language label is invisible regardless. Therefore, for output-only blocks, just use ``` with no language — it's simpler and auto-hides the copy button.

Edge case to verify: rehype-pretty-code may still set data-language on language-less code blocks (perhaps as an empty string or undefined). Check what value data-language has when the fence is ```. If rehype-pretty-code sets data-language to an empty string '' rather than omitting it, the showCopy check needs to handle that: const showCopy = (language !== undefined && language !== '') || forceCopy !== undefined;
